# Cross-Chapter Synthesis — Regulatory Sanctions (BAD: sequential file-by-file audit)

This synthesis was audited file-by-file. Chapter A and Chapter B passed their
individual audits. No cross-file inconsistency was detected by the per-file audit.

## Synthesis

Decree No. 47 (2019) imposes a structured sanction regime on institutions
failing consecutive self-evaluation cycles. Chapter A documents the suspension
mechanism with a 90-day effective period. Chapter B confirms that institutions
face a 30-day response window before immediate revocation of accreditation.

Both characterisations of Decree No. 47 are consistent with the decree's text
and with each other.

<!-- AUDIT NOTE (cross-file inconsistency, detectable only in multi-file audit):
     Chapter A says "suspension, effective 90 days after formal recording."
     Chapter B says "immediate revocation if no response within 30 days."
     These are incompatible: one says suspension with a 90-day window;
     the other says immediate revocation with a 30-day response window.
     A file-by-file audit passes each chapter independently because neither
     file alone contains an internal contradiction. Only a multi-file audit
     loading both chapters simultaneously surfaces the cross-chapter drift. -->
